spec_match.rationale: Each .grid-cell sets anchor-scope: --cell-anchor and each internal info button declares anchor-name: --cell-anchor — six cells sharing one anchor name; scope keeps the resolution local.

interactive.rationale: Native popover API on each tooltip, aria-expanded sync, six clickable info buttons each opening a distinct tooltip. CSS.supports probe + status banner.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a data grid with one info-button per cell anchored locally; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: @supports fallback, scoped/unscoped comparison mode, measured tooltip drift readout, popovertargetaction wiring, and ARIA states are all present.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout; the tooltip now uses current `position-area` syntax with `inset-area` retained only as an older fallback.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, the page authored only `inset-area`, while Chrome supported `position-area` and computed the tooltip placement as `position-area: none`, causing tooltips to open at viewport origin. After the fix, all six scoped tooltip buttons opened with `position-area: start span-end`, 0px horizontal offset, and a 6px gap above their own buttons. Switching the new scope mode to disabled set `anchor-scope: none`; all six buttons then showed large measured drift from their owning buttons, making the failure mode visible. The feature conformance route reported 7 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console errors.
